sections.heading: Core Concept
sections.content: The core idea of a linked list is: **nodes connected by pointers**.
items: Each node stores a **value** and uses **next** to point to the following node.
Nodes do not need to be contiguous in memory; pointer links define the order.
A singly linked list only moves forward; a doubly linked list adds **prev** so you can move backward.
With a **tail pointer**, appending at the end avoids walking from head to the last node.

sections.heading: Operation Complexity
sections.content: To estimate linked list operation cost, ask: **Do we already have the target node, or do we need to traverse first?**
items: **Head insert/delete**: update the head pointer directly, time complexity [[success|O(1)]].
**Tail insert**: [[success|O(1)]] with a tail pointer; [[warning|O(n)]] without one because you must traverse.
**Search / access by index**: move from head node by node; worst case [[warning|O(n)]].
**Middle insert/delete**: [[success|O(1)]] if you already have the previous node; if you only know an index, finding the position still costs [[warning|O(n)]].

sections.heading: When To Use It
sections.content: Linked lists are strong for **local rewiring, node reordering, and pointer techniques**.
items: Good for frequent insertions or deletions at the head.
Good for **fast and slow pointer** patterns: finding the middle, detecting cycles, or locating an intersection.
Good for reversing a list, merging sorted lists, and removing nodes by reconnecting links.
When data size changes often and random access is not needed, it can feel more natural than an array.

sections.heading: When To Avoid It
sections.content: Linked lists are usually not the first choice when the problem needs:
items: Frequent reads of the **k-th element**, because each access starts from the head.
Cache-friendly sequential scans; scattered nodes reduce locality.
Index-based binary search or fast jumps; arrays are usually better for that.
Small, simple data where pointer bookkeeping costs more than it helps.

animation.search_empty: Linked list is empty, cannot search
animation.search_compare: Traverse node {{index}}: is {{current}} equal to target {{target}}?
animation.search_found: Search succeeded! Found value {{value}} at node {{index}} (index {{index}})
animation.search_not_found: Search complete: value {{value}} not found in linked list
animation.insert_head_create: InsertHead({{value}}): create new node and allocate memory
animation.insert_head_link: newNode.next = head (new node points to old head {{head}})
animation.insert_head_link_prev: head.prev = newNode (old head's prev points back to new node)
animation.insert_head_update: head = newNode (update head pointer to new node)
animation.insert_head_update_tail: Linked list was empty, update tail pointer to new node
animation.insert_tail_check_null: Check if empty: head == null, true
animation.insert_tail_update_head: head = newNode (update head pointer to new node)
animation.insert_tail_update_tail_null: tail = newNode (list was empty, also update tail pointer)
animation.insert_tail_create: InsertTail({{value}}): create new node at tail and allocate memory
animation.insert_tail_link: tail.next = newNode (old tail node points to new node)
animation.insert_tail_link_prev_has_tail: newNode.prev = tail (new node's prev points back to old tail)
animation.insert_tail_pointer_move: tail = newNode (update tail pointer to new node)
animation.insert_tail_create_notail: InsertTail({{value}}): found tail, create new node and allocate memory
animation.insert_tail_link_current: current.next = newNode (last node points to new node)
animation.insert_tail_link_prev_notail: newNode.prev = current (new node's prev points back to last node)
animation.insert_index_if_tail: InsertAtIndex({{value}}, {{index}}): index equals length {{length}}, calling InsertTail
animation.insert_index_link_new_next: 2. Point new node to original Node {{N}}
animation.insert_index_link_next_prev: 2b. Original Node {{N}}'s prev points back to new node
animation.insert_index_link_new_prev: 3b. New node's prev points back to Node {{N}}
animation.insert_index_update_tail: If newNode.next = null, true — update tail
animation.delete_head_start: DeleteHead(): mark head node {{value}} for deletion
animation.delete_head_update_head: head = head.next (move head pointer to next node)
animation.delete_head_update_prev: If head ≠ null, head.prev = null (unlink new head's backward pointer)
animation.delete_head_update_tail_doubly: Else tail = null (list is now empty, clear tail)
animation.delete_head_update_tail_singly: If head = null, tail = null (list is now empty, clear tail)
animation.delete_single_start: Delete: linked list has only one node, marking for deletion
animation.delete_single_remove_with_tail: Remove node, set head and tail to null
animation.delete_single_remove: Remove node, set head to null
animation.delete_single_complete: Delete complete, linked list is now empty
animation.delete_index_if_tail: DeleteAtIndex({{value}}, {{index}}): index equals length {{length}}, calling DeleteTail
animation.delete_tail_traverse_prev: prev = tail.prev (find predecessor of tail node)
animation.delete_update_tail: tail = pre (update tail pointer to new tail node)
animation.delete_tail_traverse: Traversing: current = current.next (searching for tail node)
animation.delete_tail_unlink_doubly: current.prev.next = null (unlink through predecessor's prev pointer)
animation.delete_index_unlink: pre.next = current.next (predecessor skips target to point to next node)
animation.delete_index_unlink_prev: current.next.prev = pre (successor's prev points back to predecessor)

actionHandler.indexNegative: Invalid index: index cannot be negative
actionHandler.insertIndexOutOfRange: Index {{idx}} is out of range; maximum insert index is {{max}}
actionHandler.deleteIndexOutOfRange: Index {{idx}} is out of range; maximum delete index is {{max}}
